flow 1: home -> click su "weather" -> visualizzazione meteo della località attuale -> click su lente d'ingrandimento -> ricerca meteo di altre località
flow 2: home -> click su "weather" -> visualizzazione meteo della località attuale -> ricerca meteo di altre località
the diagram models a simple weather application flow.
the user starts at the home screen and clicks on "weather" to view the current weather.
after viewing the current weather, the user can either click on a magnifying glass icon (si) or not (no), leading to the same outcome: searching for weather in other locations.
the diagram shows a linear flow with a single decision point.

flow 1: user -> applicazione (click su "trekking") -> applicazione -> server (richiesta elenco province) -> server -> applicazione (invio elenco province) -> applicazione -> user (fornisce elenco province) -> user -> applicazione (click sulla provincia) -> applicazione -> server (richiesta elenco percorsi) -> server -> applicazione (invio elenco percorsi) -> applicazione -> user (fornisce elenco province) -> user -> applicazione (click sul percorso nella provincia selezionata)
the diagram models a sequence of interactions between a user, an application, and a server for selecting a trekking path.
the user initiates the process by clicking on "trekking" in the application.
the application then requests a list of provinces from the server, receives it, and displays it to the user.
the user selects a province, triggering a request for a list of paths within that province.
the server sends the list of paths to the application, which displays them to the user.
finally, the user selects a path.

the diagram models a use case for retrieving weather information.
the user initiates the process by clicking on a "weather" option within the application.
the application then requests weather data from a server.
the server responds by sending the weather data back to the application.
finally, the application presents the weather data to the user.
the diagram depicts a simple, linear flow without any error handling or alternative paths.

the diagram models a use case where a user interacts with an application to retrieve a list of past training sessions from a server.
the user initiates the process by clicking on the "health" option within the application.
the application then requests training data from the server.
the server responds by sending the data back to the application.
